free_text: Cyclotron production of PET radionuclides: 118Sb
(3.5 min; beta+ 75%; EC 25%) from high-energy protons
on natural Sb targets
Data compared to ALICE calculations.
External proton beams of 67.5, 60.0 and 42.2 MeV.
Energy given is average of incident and exit energy
of proton beam.
Thin targets of natural Sb (42.7% 123Sb, 57.3% 121Sb)
were prepared by using Sb2O3 powder (density 5.2
g/cm3) pressed into Al holders (0.5 mm).
Target thicknesses varied from 0.11 to 0.17 g/cm2.
Several stacks were prepared.
Stacks containing up to 20 Sb targets were irradiated
several hours with 50-100 nA beams in Faraday Cup.
Normal high resolution gamma-spectrometry method.
Nuclear data were taken from Lederer and Shirley,
Table of Isotopes, 7th Ed. (1978).
No reply from M. Lagunas-Solar to author proof.
S.Takacs
Reference added.
SD: INSTITUTE code was changed.
TS+On. Major revisions in 003,005,007,009.
See also Memo CP-D/933. STATUS modified (OS).
OS. DECAY-DATA in subs.2,3 corrected
Overall data uncertainties estimated by summing up the
individual errors in quadrature.
Relative cross section uncertainty is, generally, 11%,
but increased to 20% near threshold because of the
increased statistical error.
Data were taken from Table 3 of reference.
Decay-data for Sb-118 added
Overall data uncertainties estimated by
summing up the individual errors in quadrature.
Relative cross section uncertainty is, generally, 10%,
but increased to 20% near threshold because of the
increased statistical error.
Data were taken from text of reference.
Reaction corrected.
